rows.product_or_action_name: SES Creative — SES Creative My First Colourball
rows.ui_title: Best for fist grip colour ball
rows.ui_description: A large ball shaped crayon with six colour tips built in, made for toddler fist grips and short first scribbles on paper.
rows.why_pip_picked_this: First marks at sixteen to eighteen months are often whole hand scribbles, not neat pencil holds. The Colourball keeps six colours in one body so nothing rolls under the sofa. Best when you want one tool that invites colour changes without a fistful of separate sticks.
rows.rank_rationale: #1 over #2: single all in one grip tool; beads are separate colours on a cord.
rows.buying_factor_memo: Order follows fist grip tools first, then classic jumbo sticks, then washable and character variety. Brand caps keep SES and Crayola at two each. Buyable UK specialist and high street PDPs beat Amazon bot-wall listings.

how_trail.buying_factor_memo: Order follows fist grip tools first, then classic jumbo sticks, then washable and character variety. Brand caps keep SES and Crayola at two each. Buyable UK specialist and high street PDPs beat Amazon bot-wall listings.

rows.stage_1_card_label: The safety reset for a taller, stronger toddler
rows.ui_description: Re-check height, weight, harness position and vehicle fit before assuming the seat still fits.
rows.why_pip_picked_this: Near three, it is easy to assume the car seat is still doing its job — this is a reset check, not a shopping list.
rows.qa_note: Quick Check only — not a Pip product pick

rows.stage_1_card_label: The safety reset for a taller, stronger toddler
rows.ui_description: Check compartments on cards, remotes, scales and older-child toys; keep spares locked away.
rows.why_pip_picked_this: Birthday gifts and older siblings can bring button batteries into reach without you noticing.
rows.qa_note: Quick Check only — not a Pip product pick

rows.stage_1_card_label: The safety reset for a taller, stronger toddler
rows.ui_description: Empty paddling pools, buckets and bowls after use; keep bathroom water supervised.
rows.why_pip_picked_this: Shallow water still needs full attention as copying and reach improve.
rows.qa_note: Quick Check only — not a Pip product pick

rows.stage_1_card_label: The safety reset for a taller, stronger toddler
rows.ui_description: Check blind cords, windows and furniture they could climb to reach them.
rows.why_pip_picked_this: A taller toddler can reach things that felt safely out of range months ago.
rows.qa_note: Quick Check only — not a Pip product pick

rows.stage_1_card_label: The safety reset for a taller, stronger toddler
rows.ui_description: Check age warnings, small parts and younger siblings before gifts go in.
rows.why_pip_picked_this: Turning three changes packaging labels — it does not make every small part safe for every home.
rows.qa_note: Quick Check only — not a Pip product pick

category_how.stage_2_card_label: Soft balls to roll and chase
category_how.buying_factor_memo: Five factors set soft-ball order at 16-18m. First, chase invitation: light balls that travel invite toddle-after play over sit-and-mouth only. Second, grasp honesty for still-mouthing toddlers: silicone open designs and fabric tags beat slick heavy plastics. Third, home friction: living-room soft PVC and fabric beat outdoor-only sports foam. Fourth, distinct jobs across Top 5: free chase, grasp-squash, fabric roll, spiral drop, sound tower track. Fifth, buyable UK specialist PDPs with overlapping age marks; 18m-only tracks sit at #5 for the top of the band.
category_how.methodology: Benchmarked UK soft-ball and graspable roll options on Adventure Toys and Toytastic (Scrunch, Bigjigs silicone, Les Deglingos fabric, Quercetti large-ball runs). Captured age marks from live PDPs (birth–18m, 1y+, 18m+). Ranked with buying_factor_memo for chase distance, grasp, quiet fabric, and guided roll. URL verify via stage3-url-preflight and stage3-availability-check on 2026-07-24 (all Top 5 BUYABLE).

#3 over #4: fabric roll plus sensory tags; quieter than Scrunch for flats; trails silicone on wash-and-go grit.
